reasoning:

This means exactly 0 events occurred in the past year. Angola is largely stable, with conflict mostly limited to the FLEC insurgency in the Cabinda enclave. Furthermore, while there are civilian accidents involving unexploded ordnance (UXO) from the civil war, ACLED does not typically code purely accidental civilian UXO detonations under this event type unless they are part of active armed group hostilities. 

Given a historical base rate of 0 events per year, the probability of 1 or more events occurring in a future 30-day window is very low. Using Laplace smoothing on 0 events in 12 months suggests a baseline probability of around ~7% for a given month. 

For the earliest date (May 18, 2025), 23 days of the 30-day window have already elapsed prior to the forecast date with no reported events, making the probability for the remaining week extremely low (set at the lower allowed bound of 2%). For subsequent, fully-future 30-day periods, the probability is anchored near the theoretical 4-7% base rate, scaling up slightly (4%, 6%, 10%) to account for increasing uncertainty over the longer forecast horizon.
